  <script>
   const isSearchPage = document.body.classList.contains("searchpage");
   const isMainSite = document.body.classList.contains("main-site");
   const customSearchBox = document.querySelector('#coveo-search-btn-redirect custom-search-box');
   const mainHost = customSearchBox?.getAttribute("data-search-url");
   if (!isMainSite) {
     const searchBoxes = document.querySelectorAll('custom-search-box[data-search-url]');
     const url = new URL(window.location.href);
     const host = url.host;
     const pathSegments = url.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean);
     const isoCodeRegex = /^[a-z]{2}(-[A-Z]{2})?$/;
     const isSameHost = mainHost.includes(host);
     const searchPageHost = new URL(mainHost).host;
     const isSameDomain = (host === searchPageHost);
     let site = host;
     if (isSameHost) {
       const relevantSegment = isoCodeRegex.test(pathSegments[0]) ? pathSegments[1] : pathSegments[0] || '';
       site = host + "/" + relevantSegment;
     }
     customSearchBox.setAttribute('is-external-domain', !isSameDomain);
     searchBoxes.forEach(box => {
       const currentUrl = box.getAttribute('data-search-url');
       const newUrl = currentUrl + "?sitesearch=" + site;
       box.setAttribute('data-search-url', newUrl);
     });
   }
   if (isSearchPage) {
     const redirectEl = document.getElementById("coveo-search-btn-redirect");
     if (redirectEl) { redirectEl.remove(); }
   } else {
     const resultsEl = document.getElementById("coveo-search-btn-results");
     if (resultsEl) { resultsEl.remove(); }
   }
  </script>

<div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-c90d316038":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2026-04-16T13:02:55Z","xdm:text":"<ul>\r\n<li><i>Seven previously vacant sites will add 240 new 3-K seats, bringing programs closer to</i>&nbsp;<i>families</i>&nbsp;</li>\r\n<li><i>Additional seats for community-based providers to be announced in coming weeks</i> &nbsp;</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n"}}" id="text-c90d316038" class="cmp-text ">
    <ul><li><i>Seven previously vacant sites will add 240 new 3-K seats, bringing programs closer to</i> <i>families</i> </li><li><i>Additional seats for community-based providers to be announced in coming weeks</i>  </li></ul>

</div>

<div class="text">
<div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-7fdb61c140":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2026-04-16T13:03:33Z","xdm:text":"<p><b>NEW YORK</b>— Today, Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani and New York City Public Schools Chancellor Kamar Samuels announced seven new early childhood education centers will open this fall, adding about 240 new 3-K seats across the city. The sites, previously vacant, are part of the first phase of the 3-K expansion announced last month for the 2026–27 school year.</p>\r\n<p>The new centers will meet community demand, make full use of existing space and expand access to early childhood education closer to where families live.  </p>\r\n<p>The City also announced an expansion of capacity among existing contracted providers, adding seats across current programs. Additional center-based seats will be announced following the evaluation of the Request for Information (RFI) issued earlier this year, along with planned increases in family child care network capacity. </p>\r\n<p>“Today, we are opening doors that should never have been closed to our families — safe, nurturing spaces where their children can learn and grow, in their own communities,” said <b>Mayor Mamdani</b>. “For too long, truly accessible child care was just out of reach. By bringing these sites online, we’re making clear that no amount of red tape will get in the way of delivering the free, universal child care New Yorkers deserve.” </p>\r\n<p>“We have been deliberate about how we approach early care and education and are taking meaningful steps to put more seats closer to the families that need them most,” said <b>Schools Chancellor Kamar Samuels</b>. “These are more than just additional seats. They are the foundation every child needs to thrive, and we are committed to making sure no family in our system has to look too far to find a high-quality program for their child.”  </p>\r\n<p>The following sites will be open for the 2026–2027 school year: </p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>District 11 – 1107 East Gun Hill Road</li>\r\n<li>District 15 – 274 Atlantic Avenue</li>\r\n<li>District 22 – 1326 East 57th Street</li>\r\n<li>District 24 – 63-57 Fresh Pond Road</li>\r\n<li>District 24 – 104-72 Roosevelt Avenue</li>\r\n<li>District 24 – 47-00 76th Street</li>\r\n<li>District 25 – 18-31 131st Street</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Site selections were based on community demand, seat availability, site readiness and the ability to open for the upcoming school year.</p>\r\n<p>New programs will be listed on <a href=\"https://myschools.nyc/en/\">MySchools</a>. Families who have already applied will receive email instructions on how to update their applications. Families who missed the deadline can still join program waitlists.  </p>\r\n<p>Today’s announcement builds on the Mamdani Administration’s <a href=\"/mayors-office/news/2026/01/mayor-mamdani---governor-hochul-to-launch-free-child-care-for-tw\">commitment to delivering universal child care to New York City families</a>, supported by the state in partnership with Governor Kathy Hochul. Since Jan. 1, 2026, the City has announced the launch of <a href=\"/mayors-office/news/2026/04/mayor-mamdani-announces-2-k-will-be-full-day-and-full-year\">full-day, full-year 2-K</a>, the city’s first universal child care for 2-year-olds, <a href=\"/mayors-office/news/2026/03/mayor-mamdani-and-governor-hochul-announce-first-four-communitie\">set to launch with 2,000 initial seats this year</a>, as well as an <a href=\"/mayors-office/news/2026/03/mayor-mamdani-announces-major-3-k-expansion--adding-more-than-1-\">expansion of more than 1,000 3-K seats</a> in 56 ZIP codes.  </p>\r\n<p style=\"text-align: center;\">###</p>\r\n"}}" id="text-7fdb61c140" class="cmp-text ">
    <p><b>NEW YORK</b>— Today, Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani and New York City Public Schools Chancellor Kamar Samuels announced seven new early childhood education centers will open this fall, adding about 240 new 3-K seats across the city. The sites, previously vacant, are part of the first phase of the 3-K expansion announced last month for the 2026–27 school year.</p>
<p>The new centers will meet community demand, make full use of existing space and expand access to early childhood education closer to where families live.  </p>
<p>The City also announced an expansion of capacity among existing contracted providers, adding seats across current programs. Additional center-based seats will be announced following the evaluation of the Request for Information (RFI) issued earlier this year, along with planned increases in family child care network capacity. </p>
<p>“Today, we are opening doors that should never have been closed to our families — safe, nurturing spaces where their children can learn and grow, in their own communities,” said <b>Mayor Mamdani</b>. “For too long, truly accessible child care was just out of reach. By bringing these sites online, we’re making clear that no amount of red tape will get in the way of delivering the free, universal child care New Yorkers deserve.” </p>
<p>“We have been deliberate about how we approach early care and education and are taking meaningful steps to put more seats closer to the families that need them most,” said <b>Schools Chancellor Kamar Samuels</b>. “These are more than just additional seats. They are the foundation every child needs to thrive, and we are committed to making sure no family in our system has to look too far to find a high-quality program for their child.”  </p>
<p>The following sites will be open for the 2026–2027 school year: </p>
<ul><li>District 11 – 1107 East Gun Hill Road</li><li>District 15 – 274 Atlantic Avenue</li><li>District 22 – 1326 East 57th Street</li><li>District 24 – 63-57 Fresh Pond Road</li><li>District 24 – 104-72 Roosevelt Avenue</li><li>District 24 – 47-00 76th Street</li><li>District 25 – 18-31 131st Street</li></ul>
<p>Site selections were based on community demand, seat availability, site readiness and the ability to open for the upcoming school year.</p>
<p>New programs will be listed on <a href="https://myschools.nyc/en/">MySchools</a>. Families who have already applied will receive email instructions on how to update their applications. Families who missed the deadline can still join program waitlists.  </p>
<p>Today’s announcement builds on the Mamdani Administration’s <a href="/mayors-office/news/2026/01/mayor-mamdani---governor-hochul-to-launch-free-child-care-for-tw">commitment to delivering universal child care to New York City families</a>, supported by the state in partnership with Governor Kathy Hochul. Since Jan. 1, 2026, the City has announced the launch of <a href="/mayors-office/news/2026/04/mayor-mamdani-announces-2-k-will-be-full-day-and-full-year">full-day, full-year 2-K</a>, the city’s first universal child care for 2-year-olds, <a href="/mayors-office/news/2026/03/mayor-mamdani-and-governor-hochul-announce-first-four-communitie">set to launch with 2,000 initial seats this year</a>, as well as an <a href="/mayors-office/news/2026/03/mayor-mamdani-announces-major-3-k-expansion--adding-more-than-1-">expansion of more than 1,000 3-K seats</a> in 56 ZIP codes.  </p>
<p style="	text-align: center;
">###</p>

</div>

    

</div>

gold_text:
Mayor Mamdani Announces Seven New Early Childhood Education Centers Opening This Fall
What you should know
Seven previously vacant sites will add 240 new 3-K seats, bringing programs closer to families 
Additional seats for community-based providers to be announced in coming weeks  

NEW YORK— Today, Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani and New York City Public Schools Chancellor Kamar Samuels announced seven new early childhood education centers will open this fall, adding about 240 new 3-K seats across the city. The sites, previously vacant, are part of the first phase of the 3-K expansion announced last month for the 2026–27 school year.
The new centers will meet community demand, make full use of existing space and expand access to early childhood education closer to where families live.  
The City also announced an expansion of capacity among existing contracted providers, adding seats across current programs. Additional center-based seats will be announced following the evaluation of the Request for Information (RFI) issued earlier this year, along with planned increases in family child care network capacity. 
“Today, we are opening doors that should never have been closed to our families — safe, nurturing spaces where their children can learn and grow, in their own communities,” said Mayor Mamdani. “For too long, truly accessible child care was just out of reach. By bringing these sites online, we’re making clear that no amount of red tape will get in the way of delivering the free, universal child care New Yorkers deserve.” 
“We have been deliberate about how we approach early care and education and are taking meaningful steps to put more seats closer to the families that need them most,” said Schools Chancellor Kamar Samuels. “These are more than just additional seats. They are the foundation every child needs to thrive, and we are committed to making sure no family in our system has to look too far to find a high-quality program for their child.”  

The following sites will be open for the 2026–2027 school year: 
District 11 – 1107 East Gun Hill Road
District 15 – 274 Atlantic Avenue
District 22 – 1326 East 57th Street
District 24 – 63-57 Fresh Pond Road
District 24 – 104-72 Roosevelt Avenue
District 24 – 47-00 76th Street
District 25 – 18-31 131st Street

Site selections were based on community demand, seat availability, site readiness and the ability to open for the upcoming school year.
New programs will be listed on MySchools. Families who have already applied will receive email instructions on how to update their applications. Families who missed the deadline can still join program waitlists.  
Today’s announcement builds on the Mamdani Administration’s commitment to delivering universal child care to New York City families, supported by the state in partnership with Governor Kathy Hochul. Since Jan. 1, 2026, the City has announced the launch of full-day, full-year 2-K, the city’s first universal child care for 2-year-olds, set to launch with 2,000 initial seats this year, as well as an expansion of more than 1,000 3-K seats in 56 ZIP codes.

            <script>
   const isSearchPage = document.body.classList.contains("searchpage");
   const isMainSite = document.body.classList.contains("main-site");
   const customSearchBox = document.querySelector('#coveo-search-btn-redirect custom-search-box');
   const mainHost = customSearchBox?.getAttribute("data-search-url");
   if (!isMainSite) {
     const searchBoxes = document.querySelectorAll('custom-search-box[data-search-url]');
     const url = new URL(window.location.href);
     const host = url.host;
     const pathSegments = url.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean);
     const isoCodeRegex = /^[a-z]{2}(-[A-Z]{2})?$/;
     const isSameHost = mainHost.includes(host);
     const searchPageHost = new URL(mainHost).host;
     const isSameDomain = (host === searchPageHost);
     let site = host;
     if (isSameHost) {
       const relevantSegment = isoCodeRegex.test(pathSegments[0]) ? pathSegments[1] : pathSegments[0] || '';
       site = host + "/" + relevantSegment;
     }
     customSearchBox.setAttribute('is-external-domain', !isSameDomain);
     searchBoxes.forEach(box => {
       const currentUrl = box.getAttribute('data-search-url');
       const newUrl = currentUrl + "?sitesearch=" + site;
       box.setAttribute('data-search-url', newUrl);
     });
   }
   if (isSearchPage) {
     const redirectEl = document.getElementById("coveo-search-btn-redirect");
     if (redirectEl) { redirectEl.remove(); }
   } else {
     const resultsEl = document.getElementById("coveo-search-btn-results");
     if (resultsEl) { resultsEl.remove(); }
   }
  </script>

            <div class="title">
             <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"title-c82cd95a8c":{"@type":"nycgov/components/title","repo:modifyDate":"2024-10-21T22:02:46Z","dc:title":"Pay ticket"}}" id="title-c82cd95a8c" class="cmp-title">
              <h2 class="cmp-title__text" id="pay-ticket">Pay ticket</h2>
             </div>
            </div>
            <div class="text">
             <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-5f0c36e44e":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2026-04-03T14:14:14Z","xdm:text":"<p>If you received any of the following in NYC...</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Parking ticket</li>\r\n<li>School zone speed camera violation</li>\r\n<li>Red light camera violation</li>\r\n<li>Bus lane camera violation</li>\r\n<li>MTA bus camera violation</li>\r\n<li>Weigh-in-motion camera violation</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>You must pay your fine within 30 days to avoid penalties.</p>\r\n"}}" id="text-5f0c36e44e" class="cmp-text ">
              <p>If you received any of the following in NYC...</p>
              <ul>
               <li>Parking ticket</li>
               <li>School zone speed camera violation</li>
               <li>Red light camera violation</li>
               <li>Bus lane camera violation</li>
               <li>MTA bus camera violation</li>
               <li>Weigh-in-motion camera violation</li>
              </ul>
              <p>You must pay your fine within 30 days to avoid penalties.</p>
             </div>
            </div>
            <div class="nyc-you-should-know">
             <div class="nyc-you-should-know__header">
              <div class="nyc-you-should-know__icon">
               <svg width="16" height="21" viewbox="0 0 16 21" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><path d="M6 20H10M4 12.7H12M1 7.3C1 5.62914 1.7375 4.02671 3.05025 2.84523C4.36301 1.66375 6.14349 1 8 1C9.85652 1 11.637 1.66375 12.9497 2.84523C14.2625 4.02671 15 5.62914 15 7.3C15.0008 8.31439 14.7283 9.31384 14.2058 10.2126C13.6833 11.1115 12.9264 11.8828 12 12.4606L11.458 14.77C11.3862 15.1957 11.1473 15.5841 10.7846 15.8648C10.4219 16.1455 9.95938 16.2999 9.481 16.3H6.519C6.04062 16.2999 5.57811 16.1455 5.21539 15.8648C4.85267 15.5841 4.61376 15.1957 4.542 14.77L4 12.4705C3.07329 11.8907 2.31636 11.1175 1.79393 10.2171C1.27149 9.31667 0.999088 8.31579 1 7.3Z" stroke="black" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" />
               </svg>
              </div> <span class="h4">You should know </span>
             </div>
             <div class="cmp-text">
              <p>It takes time for tickets to appear in the system. If you don’t see your ticket, check back in a few days. You can also pay in advance by entering your violation number.</p>
             </div>
            </div>

              </div>
              <div class="button cmp-button--secondary"><a id="button-a7ea6cf7d2" class="cmp-button" data-cmp-clickable data-cmp-data-layer="{"button-a7ea6cf7d2":{"@type":"nycgov/components/button","repo:modifyDate":"2024-11-20T19:55:03Z","dc:title":"Pay with mobile app","xdm:linkURL":"https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/vehicles/nyc-pay-or-dispute.page"}}" href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/vehicles/nyc-pay-or-dispute.page"> <span class="cmp-button__text">Pay with mobile app</span> </a>
              </div>

            <div class="text">
             <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-853b1a1fc8":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2024-11-20T19:55:43Z","xdm:text":"<p><a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/pay-now/mail.page\">Learn how to pay by mail</a></p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/pay-now/person.page\">Learn how to pay in person</a></p>\r\n"}}" id="text-853b1a1fc8" class="cmp-text ">
              <p><a href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/pay-now/mail.page">Learn how to pay by mail</a></p>
              <p><a href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/pay-now/person.page">Learn how to pay in person</a></p>

              <h2 class="cmp-title__text" id="dispute-ticket">Dispute ticket</h2>
             </div>
            </div>
            <div class="text">
             <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-83090c1b08":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2024-10-21T22:10:46Z","xdm:text":"<p>If you want to dispute a ticket, request a hearing within 30 days to avoid penalties.</p>\r\n<p>You can complete the dispute process entirely online or by mail. You don’t need to appear in person.&nbsp;</p>\r\n"}}" id="text-83090c1b08" class="cmp-text ">
              <p>If you want to dispute a ticket, request a hearing within 30 days to avoid penalties.</p>
              <p>You can complete the dispute process entirely online or by mail. You don’t need to appear in person. </p>
             </div>

              <p><a href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/vehicles/dispute-mail.page">Learn how to dispute by mail</a></p>

            <div class="title">
             <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"title-fa22c4d7e7":{"@type":"nycgov/components/title","repo:modifyDate":"2024-10-21T22:03:25Z","dc:title":"Check ticket status"}}" id="title-fa22c4d7e7" class="cmp-title">
              <h2 class="cmp-title__text" id="check-ticket-status">Check ticket status</h2>
             </div>
            </div>
            <div class="text">
             <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-228f5c6689":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2026-04-03T14:14:44Z","xdm:text":"<p>You can check the status of your parking or camera violation payment, dispute, or appeal online.</p>\r\n<p>You can also:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Check whether your vehicles have other tickets</li>\r\n<li>See the total amount you owe</li>\r\n<li>Get a copy of your parking ticket</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n"}}" id="text-228f5c6689" class="cmp-text ">
              <p>You can check the status of your parking or camera violation payment, dispute, or appeal online.</p>
              <p>You can also:</p>
              <ul>
               <li>Check whether your vehicles have other tickets</li>
               <li>See the total amount you owe</li>
               <li>Get a copy of your parking ticket</li>
              </ul>
             </div>
            </div>
            <div class="button cmp-button--primary"><a id="button-6e375667a1" class="cmp-button" data-cmp-clickable data-cmp-data-layer="{"button-6e375667a1":{"@type":"nycgov/components/button","repo:modifyDate":"2024-11-20T19:59:38Z","dc:title":"Check ticket status online","xdm:linkURL":"https://nycserv.nyc.gov/NYCServWeb/PVO_Search.jsp"}}" href="https://nycserv.nyc.gov/NYCServWeb/PVO_Search.jsp"> <span class="cmp-button__text">Check ticket status online</span> </a>
            </div>
           </div>

              <div class="cmp-accordion__item" data-cmp-hook-accordion="item" data-cmp-data-layer="{"accordion-545dc48da3-item-0fb8d55982":{"@type":"core/wcm/components/accordion/v1/accordion/item","dc:title":"What happens if I don’t pay?"}}" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-0fb8d55982">
               <div class="cmp-accordion__header">
                <h3><button id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-0fb8d55982-button" class="cmp-accordion__button" type="button" aria-controls="accordion-545dc48da3-item-0fb8d55982-panel" data-cmp-hook-accordion="button"> <span class="cmp-accordion__icon"></span> <span class="cmp-accordion__title">What happens if I don’t pay?</span> </button></h3>
               </div>
               <div data-cmp-hook-accordion="panel" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-0fb8d55982-panel" class="cmp-accordion__panel cmp-accordion__panel--hidden" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-545dc48da3-item-0fb8d55982-button">
                <div class="container responsivegrid">
                 <div id="container-0fb8d55982" class="cmp-container">
                  <div class="aem-Grid aem-Grid--12 aem-Grid--default--12 ">
                   <div class="container responsivegrid aem-GridColumn aem-GridColumn--default--12">
                    <div id="container-70e0aa82e0" class="cmp-container">
                     <div class="text">
                      <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-61307351fc":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2026-04-07T18:30:06Z","xdm:text":"<p>If you don’t pay or dispute your fine within 30 days, you will be charged late fees. Refer to the tables below for penalty schedules. If your ticket is entered <a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/vehicles/services-tickets-in-judgment.page\">into judgment</a>, you will be charged interest and your vehicle may be booted or towed.</p>\r\n<h4>Unpaid parking ticket penalties</h4>\r\n<div class=\"nyc-table\"><table>\r\n<tbody><tr><th><p>Time since violation issued</p>\r\n</th>\r\n<th><p>Penalty</p>\r\n</th>\r\n</tr><tr><td><p>Less than 30 days</p>\r\n</td>\r\n<td><p>None</p>\r\n</td>\r\n</tr><tr><td><p>30 days</p>\r\n</td>\r\n<td><p>$10</p>\r\n</td>\r\n</tr><tr><td><p>60 days</p>\r\n</td>\r\n<td><p>$20, plus previous penalty</p>\r\n</td>\r\n</tr><tr><td><p>90 days</p>\r\n</td>\r\n<td><p>$30, plus previous penalties</p>\r\n</td>\r\n</tr><tr><td><p>100 days</p>\r\n</td>\r\n<td><p>Ticket entered into judgment</p>\r\n</td>\r\n</tr></tbody></table></div>\r\n<h4>Unpaid camera violation penalties</h4>\r\n<div class=\"nyc-table\"><table>\r\n<tbody><tr><th><p>Time since NOL issued</p>\r\n</th>\r\n<th><p>Penalty</p>\r\n</th>\r\n</tr><tr><td><p>Less than 30 days</p>\r\n</td>\r\n<td><p>None</p>\r\n</td>\r\n</tr><tr><td><p>30 days</p>\r\n</td>\r\n<td><p>$25</p>\r\n</td>\r\n</tr><tr><td><p>65 days</p>\r\n</td>\r\n<td><p>Final notice mailed</p>\r\n</td>\r\n</tr><tr><td>75 days</td>\r\n<td>Ticket entered into judgment</td>\r\n</tr></tbody></table></div>\r\n"}}" id="text-61307351fc" class="cmp-text ">
                       <p>If you don’t pay or dispute your fine within 30 days, you will be charged late fees. Refer to the tables below for penalty schedules. If your ticket is entered <a href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/vehicles/services-tickets-in-judgment.page">into judgment</a>, you will be charged interest and your vehicle may be booted or towed.</p>
                       <h4>Unpaid parking ticket penalties</h4>
                       <div class="nyc-table">
                        <table>
                         <tbody>
                          <tr>
                           <th><p>Time since violation issued</p></th>
                           <th><p>Penalty</p></th>
                          </tr>
                          <tr>
                           <td><p>Less than 30 days</p></td>
                           <td><p>None</p></td>
                          </tr>
                          <tr>
                           <td><p>30 days</p></td>
                           <td><p>$10</p></td>
                          </tr>
                          <tr>
                           <td><p>60 days</p></td>
                           <td><p>$20, plus previous penalty</p></td>
                          </tr>
                          <tr>
                           <td><p>90 days</p></td>
                           <td><p>$30, plus previous penalties</p></td>
                          </tr>
                          <tr>
                           <td><p>100 days</p></td>
                           <td><p>Ticket entered into judgment</p></td>
                          </tr>
                         </tbody>
                        </table>
                       </div>
                       <h4>Unpaid camera violation penalties</h4>
                       <div class="nyc-table">
                        <table>
                         <tbody>
                          <tr>
                           <th><p>Time since NOL issued</p></th>
                           <th><p>Penalty</p></th>
                          </tr>
                          <tr>
                           <td><p>Less than 30 days</p></td>
                           <td><p>None</p></td>
                          </tr>
                          <tr>
                           <td><p>30 days</p></td>
                           <td><p>$25</p></td>
                          </tr>
                          <tr>
                           <td><p>65 days</p></td>
                           <td><p>Final notice mailed</p></td>
                          </tr>
                          <tr>
                           <td>75 days</td>
                           <td>Ticket entered into judgment</td>
                          </tr>
                         </tbody>
                        </table>
                       </div>
                      </div>
                     </div>
                     <div class="text">
                     </div>
                    </div>
                   </div>
                  </div>
                 </div>
                </div>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="cmp-accordion__item" data-cmp-hook-accordion="item" data-cmp-data-layer="{"accordion-545dc48da3-item-7f1eada520":{"@type":"core/wcm/components/accordion/v1/accordion/item","dc:title":"Why can’t I find my ticket information?"}}" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-7f1eada520">
               <div class="cmp-accordion__header">
                <h3><button id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-7f1eada520-button" class="cmp-accordion__button" type="button" aria-controls="accordion-545dc48da3-item-7f1eada520-panel" data-cmp-hook-accordion="button"> <span class="cmp-accordion__icon"></span> <span class="cmp-accordion__title">Why can’t I find my ticket information?</span> </button></h3>
               </div>
               <div data-cmp-hook-accordion="panel" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-7f1eada520-panel" class="cmp-accordion__panel cmp-accordion__panel--hidden" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-545dc48da3-item-7f1eada520-button">
                <div class="container responsivegrid">
                 <div id="container-7f1eada520" class="cmp-container">
                  <div class="container responsivegrid">
                   <div id="container-097101a617" class="cmp-container">
                    <div class="text">
                     <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-aef99a9871":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2025-05-30T16:31:16Z","xdm:text":"<p>It can take up to three weeks for your ticket to appear in the online system. </p>\r\n<p>You can pay your ticket before it appears by entering the violation number and fine amount from the paper ticket <a href=\"https://a836-citypay.nyc.gov/citypay/Parking?stage=procurement#!/by-violation-form\">into CityPay</a>. Please note that you can only pay in advance by searching the violation number, not your license plate number.&nbsp;</p>\r\n<p>This information is only for NYC parking or camera tickets. If you received a moving violation ticket from a police officer, you must <a href=\"https://dmv.ny.gov/tickets/how-plead-or-pay-nyc-tvb-traffic-tickets\">pay through the DMV.</a></p>\r\n"}}" id="text-aef99a9871" class="cmp-text ">
                      <p>It can take up to three weeks for your ticket to appear in the online system.</p>
                      <p>You can pay your ticket before it appears by entering the violation number and fine amount from the paper ticket <a href="https://a836-citypay.nyc.gov/citypay/Parking?stage=procurement#!/by-violation-form">into CityPay</a>. Please note that you can only pay in advance by searching the violation number, not your license plate number. </p>
                      <p>This information is only for NYC parking or camera tickets. If you received a moving violation ticket from a police officer, you must <a href="https://dmv.ny.gov/tickets/how-plead-or-pay-nyc-tvb-traffic-tickets">pay through the DMV.</a></p>
                     </div>
                    </div>
                   </div>
                  </div>
                 </div>
                </div>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="cmp-accordion__item" data-cmp-hook-accordion="item" data-cmp-data-layer="{"accordion-545dc48da3-item-8fdac5af02":{"@type":"core/wcm/components/accordion/v1/accordion/item","dc:title":"How does disputing work?"}}" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-8fdac5af02">
               <div class="cmp-accordion__header">
                <h3><button id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-8fdac5af02-button" class="cmp-accordion__button" type="button" aria-controls="accordion-545dc48da3-item-8fdac5af02-panel" data-cmp-hook-accordion="button"> <span class="cmp-accordion__icon"></span> <span class="cmp-accordion__title">How does disputing work?</span> </button></h3>
               </div>
               <div data-cmp-hook-accordion="panel" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-8fdac5af02-panel" class="cmp-accordion__panel cmp-accordion__panel--hidden" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-545dc48da3-item-8fdac5af02-button">
                <div class="container responsivegrid">
                 <div id="container-8fdac5af02" class="cmp-container">
                  <div class="container responsivegrid">
                   <div id="container-181a616599" class="cmp-container">
                    <div class="text">
                     <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-2b7c6ebebd":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2024-12-09T19:39:22Z","xdm:text":"<ol>\r\n<li><p>You request a hearing and submit evidence.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>An administrative judge reviews your case and decides whether to dismiss your ticket.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>If your ticket is dismissed, you do not have to pay.&nbsp;</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>If your ticket is not dismissed, you’ll have to pay the fine and any penalties charged before you requested a hearing.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n"}}" id="text-2b7c6ebebd" class="cmp-text ">
                      <ol>
                       <li><p>You request a hearing and submit evidence.</p></li>
                       <li><p>An administrative judge reviews your case and decides whether to dismiss your ticket.</p></li>
                       <li><p>If your ticket is dismissed, you do not have to pay. </p></li>
                       <li><p>If your ticket is not dismissed, you’ll have to pay the fine and any penalties charged before you requested a hearing.</p></li>
                      </ol>
                     </div>
                    </div>
                   </div>
                  </div>
                 </div>
                </div>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="cmp-accordion__item" data-cmp-hook-accordion="item" data-cmp-data-layer="{"accordion-545dc48da3-item-6915cd7cff":{"@type":"core/wcm/components/accordion/v1/accordion/item","dc:title":"I paid my ticket, why does it still say I owe money?"}}" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-6915cd7cff">
               <div class="cmp-accordion__header">
                <h3><button id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-6915cd7cff-button" class="cmp-accordion__button" type="button" aria-controls="accordion-545dc48da3-item-6915cd7cff-panel" data-cmp-hook-accordion="button"> <span class="cmp-accordion__icon"></span> <span class="cmp-accordion__title">I paid my ticket, why does it still say I owe money?</span> </button></h3>
               </div>
               <div data-cmp-hook-accordion="panel" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-6915cd7cff-panel" class="cmp-accordion__panel cmp-accordion__panel--hidden" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-545dc48da3-item-6915cd7cff-button">
                <div class="container responsivegrid">
                 <div id="container-6915cd7cff" class="cmp-container">
                  <div class="container responsivegrid">
                   <div id="container-f5d5596b30" class="cmp-container">
                    <div class="text">
                     <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-3a234d6d60":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2026-04-07T18:46:53Z","xdm:text":"<p>Payments can take up to two weeks to be processed and show up in the system.&nbsp;Check your bank or credit card account to confirm funds were withdrawn.</p>\r\n<p>If you’ve paid in full and the ticket still appears after two weeks, <a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/assets/finance/jump/crm/contact-parking-ticket-payments.html\">submit an online request for assistance</a>.</p>\r\n<p>&nbsp;</p>\r\n"}}" id="text-3a234d6d60" class="cmp-text ">
                      <p>Payments can take up to two weeks to be processed and show up in the system. Check your bank or credit card account to confirm funds were withdrawn.</p>
                      <p>If you’ve paid in full and the ticket still appears after two weeks, <a href="https://www.nyc.gov/assets/finance/jump/crm/contact-parking-ticket-payments.html">submit an online request for assistance</a>.</p>
                      <p> </p>
                     </div>
                    </div>
                   </div>
                  </div>
                 </div>
                </div>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="cmp-accordion__item" data-cmp-hook-accordion="item" data-cmp-data-layer="{"accordion-545dc48da3-item-a5967c751b":{"@type":"core/wcm/components/accordion/v1/accordion/item","dc:title":"Are there fees for paying a ticket?"}}" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-a5967c751b">
               <div class="cmp-accordion__header">
                <h3><button id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-a5967c751b-button" class="cmp-accordion__button" type="button" aria-controls="accordion-545dc48da3-item-a5967c751b-panel" data-cmp-hook-accordion="button"> <span class="cmp-accordion__icon"></span> <span class="cmp-accordion__title">Are there fees for paying a ticket?</span> </button></h3>
               </div>
               <div data-cmp-hook-accordion="panel" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-a5967c751b-panel" class="cmp-accordion__panel cmp-accordion__panel--hidden" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-545dc48da3-item-a5967c751b-button">
                <div class="container responsivegrid">
                 <div id="container-a5967c751b" class="cmp-container">
                  <div class="container responsivegrid">
                   <div id="container-fbc4cb79db" class="cmp-container">
                    <div class="text">
                     <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-9aa26febc9":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2024-12-09T19:40:32Z","xdm:text":"<p>There is a 2% processing fee if you pay with a credit card, debit card, Venmo, or PayPal. There are no fees for paying by check, electronic check, or money order.&nbsp;</p>\r\n<p>Watch out for private companies that charge extra fees for paying your ticket. Paying directly is the safest and most affordable option.</p>\r\n"}}" id="text-9aa26febc9" class="cmp-text ">
                      <p>There is a 2% processing fee if you pay with a credit card, debit card, Venmo, or PayPal. There are no fees for paying by check, electronic check, or money order. </p>
                      <p>Watch out for private companies that charge extra fees for paying your ticket. Paying directly is the safest and most affordable option.</p>
                     </div>
                    </div>
                   </div>
                  </div>
                 </div>
                </div>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="cmp-accordion__item" data-cmp-hook-accordion="item" data-cmp-data-layer="{"accordion-545dc48da3-item-22b370b675":{"@type":"core/wcm/components/accordion/v1/accordion/item","dc:title":"Where can I get a copy of my camera ticket?"}}" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-22b370b675">
               <div class="cmp-accordion__header">
                <h3><button id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-22b370b675-button" class="cmp-accordion__button" type="button" aria-controls="accordion-545dc48da3-item-22b370b675-panel" data-cmp-hook-accordion="button"> <span class="cmp-accordion__icon"></span> <span class="cmp-accordion__title">Where can I get a copy of my camera ticket?</span> </button></h3>
               </div>
               <div data-cmp-hook-accordion="panel" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-22b370b675-panel" class="cmp-accordion__panel cmp-accordion__panel--hidden" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-545dc48da3-item-22b370b675-button">
                <div class="container responsivegrid">
                 <div id="container-22b370b675" class="cmp-container">
                  <div class="text">
                   <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-1474732914":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2026-04-07T19:03:42Z","xdm:text":"<p>To obtain MTA Bus, red light, and speed camera violation NOL copies, contact 311,&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/assets/finance/jump/crm/contact-parking-ticket-copy-request.html\">submit a request online</a>, or visit a <a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/about/contact-us.page\">DOF Business Center</a>.</p>\r\n<p>You can <a href=\"https://nycserv.nyc.gov/NYCServWeb/PVO_Search.jsp\">access violation information</a> and review images and video online.</p>\r\n"}}" id="text-1474732914" class="cmp-text ">
                    <p>To obtain MTA Bus, red light, and speed camera violation NOL copies, contact 311, <a href="https://www.nyc.gov/assets/finance/jump/crm/contact-parking-ticket-copy-request.html">submit a request online</a>, or visit a <a href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/about/contact-us.page">DOF Business Center</a>.</p>
                    <p>You can <a href="https://nycserv.nyc.gov/NYCServWeb/PVO_Search.jsp">access violation information</a> and review images and video online.</p>
                   </div>
                  </div>
                 </div>
                </div>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="cmp-accordion__item" data-cmp-hook-accordion="item" data-cmp-data-layer="{"accordion-545dc48da3-item-3afa982b4e":{"@type":"core/wcm/components/accordion/v1/accordion/item","dc:title":"Where can I get a copy of my hearing decision?"}}" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-3afa982b4e">
               <div class="cmp-accordion__header">
                <h3><button id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-3afa982b4e-button" class="cmp-accordion__button" type="button" aria-controls="accordion-545dc48da3-item-3afa982b4e-panel" data-cmp-hook-accordion="button"> <span class="cmp-accordion__icon"></span> <span class="cmp-accordion__title">Where can I get a copy of my hearing decision?</span> </button></h3>
               </div>
               <div data-cmp-hook-accordion="panel" id="accordion-545dc48da3-item-3afa982b4e-panel" class="cmp-accordion__panel cmp-accordion__panel--hidden" role="region" aria-labelledby="accordion-545dc48da3-item-3afa982b4e-button">
                <div class="container responsivegrid">
                 <div id="container-3afa982b4e" class="cmp-container">
                  <div class="text">
                   <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-89f5550c8e":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2026-04-07T18:44:56Z","xdm:text":"<p><b>By mail:</b>&nbsp;Send a written request including your name, address, plate number, and ticket number to:</p>\r\n<p style=\"margin-left: 40.0px;\">NYC Department of Finance, Correspondence Unit,&nbsp;One Centre Street, 22nd Floor,&nbsp;New York, NY 10007.</p>\r\n<p><b>By phone:</b>&nbsp;Call <a href=\"tel:311\">311</a> or 212-NEW-YORK (<a href=\"tel:212-639-9675\">212-639-9675</a>).</p>\r\n<p><b>Online:</b>&nbsp;Use the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://nycserv.nyc.gov/NYCServWeb/PVO_Search.jsp\">NYC Serv Website&nbsp;to look up status</a>.</p>\r\n<p><b>Disputed decision:</b>&nbsp;If you already had a hearing but never received the decision, contact the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/vehicles/opsa.page\">Parking Summons Advocate</a>&nbsp;for assistance.</p>\r\n"}}" id="text-89f5550c8e" class="cmp-text ">
                    <p><b>By mail:</b> Send a written request including your name, address, plate number, and ticket number to:</p>
                    <p style="	margin-left: 40.0px;
">NYC Department of Finance, Correspondence Unit, One Centre Street, 22nd Floor, New York, NY 10007.</p>
                    <p><b>By phone:</b> Call <a href="tel:311">311</a> or 212-NEW-YORK (<a href="tel:212-639-9675">212-639-9675</a>).</p>
                    <p><b>Online:</b> Use the <a href="https://nycserv.nyc.gov/NYCServWeb/PVO_Search.jsp">NYC Serv Website to look up status</a>.</p>
                    <p><b>Disputed decision:</b> If you already had a hearing but never received the decision, contact the <a href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/vehicles/opsa.page">Parking Summons Advocate</a> for assistance.</p>
                   </div>
                  </div>
                 </div>
                </div>
               </div>
              </div>
             </div>
            </div>
           </div>
          </div>
          <div class="separator">
           <div id="separator-a10fe96798" class="cmp-separator">
            <hr class="cmp-separator__horizontal-rule">
           </div>
          </div>
          <div class="container responsivegrid">
           <div id="container-20dad6d6fa" class="cmp-container">
            <div class="container responsivegrid cmp-container--surface flow flow--space-fluid-lg-xl">
             <div id="container-614423008f" class="cmp-container">
              <div class="title">
               <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"title-37b1159f14":{"@type":"nycgov/components/title","repo:modifyDate":"2024-12-09T19:55:04Z","dc:title":"Get help"}}" id="title-37b1159f14" class="cmp-title">
                <h2 class="cmp-title__text" id="get-help">Get help</h2>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="title">
               <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"title-799bdcfb32":{"@type":"nycgov/components/title","repo:modifyDate":"2024-12-05T18:16:56Z","dc:title":"By phone"}}" id="title-799bdcfb32" class="cmp-title">
                <h3 class="cmp-title__text">By phone</h3>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="text">
               <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-f28d1fd0c9":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2025-03-20T19:56:12Z","xdm:text":"<p>Dial <a href=\"tel:311\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\">311</a> if you’re in NYC or 212-NEW-YORK&nbsp;<a href=\"tel:2126399675\">(212-639-9675)</a>&nbsp;if you’re outside of NYC.</p>\r\n"}}" id="text-f28d1fd0c9" class="cmp-text ">
                <p>Dial <a href="tel:311" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">311</a> if you’re in NYC or 212-NEW-YORK <a href="tel:2126399675">(212-639-9675)</a> if you’re outside of NYC.</p>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="title">
               <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"title-8062e6c061":{"@type":"nycgov/components/title","repo:modifyDate":"2024-10-21T22:17:17Z","dc:title":"Online"}}" id="title-8062e6c061" class="cmp-title">
                <h3 class="cmp-title__text">Online</h3>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="text">
               <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-0c1e4f96e3":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2025-04-14T14:42:46Z","xdm:text":"<p>Contact the Department of Finance (DOF).</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/about/contact-us.page\">Contact DOF online</a></p>\r\n"}}" id="text-0c1e4f96e3" class="cmp-text ">
                <p>Contact the Department of Finance (DOF).</p>
                <p><a href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/about/contact-us.page">Contact DOF online</a></p>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="title">
               <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"title-6349134b71":{"@type":"nycgov/components/title","repo:modifyDate":"2024-10-21T22:17:33Z","dc:title":"In person"}}" id="title-6349134b71" class="cmp-title">
                <h3 class="cmp-title__text">In person</h3>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="text">
               <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-d758100889":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2025-04-14T14:43:32Z","xdm:text":"<p>Visit a DOF business center.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/about/contact-us.page\">Find a business center</a></p>\r\n"}}" id="text-d758100889" class="cmp-text ">
                <p>Visit a DOF business center.</p>
                <p><a href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/about/contact-us.page">Find a business center</a></p>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="title">
               <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"title-c79193e0df":{"@type":"nycgov/components/title","repo:modifyDate":"2024-10-21T22:17:56Z","dc:title":"Parking Summons Advocate"}}" id="title-c79193e0df" class="cmp-title">
                <h3 class="cmp-title__text">Parking Summons Advocate</h3>
               </div>
              </div>
              <div class="text">
               <div data-cmp-data-layer="{"text-8e5b1e6f8f":{"@type":"nycgov/components/text","repo:modifyDate":"2025-03-20T18:58:58Z","xdm:text":"<p>If you’ve tried to resolve a ticket issue with DOF and still need help, you can contact the Office of the Parking Summons Advocate.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/vehicles/opsa.page\">Parking Summons Advocate</a></p>\r\n"}}" id="text-8e5b1e6f8f" class="cmp-text ">
                <p>If you’ve tried to resolve a ticket issue with DOF and still need help, you can contact the Office of the Parking Summons Advocate.</p>
                <p><a href="https://www.nyc.gov/site/finance/vehicles/opsa.page">Parking Summons Advocate</a></p>
               </div>
              </div>
             </div>
            </div>
           </div>
          </div>

           <div id="separator-1213f50f22" class="cmp-separator">
            <hr class="cmp-separator__horizontal-rule">
           </div>
          </div>

gold_text:
NYC parking or camera tickets
Pay ticket
If you received any of the following in NYC...

Parking ticket
School zone speed camera violation
Red light camera violation
Bus lane camera violation
MTA bus camera violation
Weigh-in-motion camera violation
You must pay your fine within 30 days to avoid penalties.

You should know
It takes time for tickets to appear in the system. If you don’t see your ticket, check back in a few days. You can also pay in advance by entering your violation number.

Dispute ticket
If you want to dispute a ticket, request a hearing within 30 days to avoid penalties.
You can complete the dispute process entirely online or by mail. You don’t need to appear in person. 

Check ticket status
You can check the status of your parking or camera violation payment, dispute, or appeal online.
You can also:
Check whether your vehicles have other tickets
See the total amount you owe
Get a copy of your parking ticket

title: Electronics and E-Waste
html_text:

  • Oils, Grease, & Fats
  • Plant, Leaf, & Yard Waste
Share
Print

Electronics and E-Waste

It’s ILLEGAL to dispose of certain electronic devices with trash or recycling. Some electronics contain harmful materials and are toxic to the environment.

Types of E-Waste

Only certain electronic items qualify as e-waste under the NYS Electronic Equipment Recycling and Reuse Act. Check the full list of items covered by the act.

Computers

  • Desktop towers
  • Laptops
  • Small-scale servers

Computer accessories (peripherals)

  • Monitors
  • Electronic keyboards, mice and pointing devices
  • Printers, scanners, and fax machines
  • External hard drives

TV and video equipment

  • Televisions
  • VCRs, DVD players, and DVRs
  • Cable boxes, digital converters, and satellite receivers
  • Cathode ray tubes

Portable electronic devices

  • Cell phones, tablets, and e-readers
  • Portal digital music players

Home electronics

  • Electronic or video game consoles (including handheld)
  • Virtual reality headsets (with processors)
  • Digital picture frames

NOTE: Items covered under New York State’s Electronic Equipment and Telephone Recycling Acts DO NOT include most electronic household appliances (microwave ovens, refrigerators), digital cameras, radios, calculators, GPS devices, power tools, medical equipment, or batteries. Though they do not qualify as e-waste, they may still have special disposal rules.

Disposal Options

Residents

To get rid of e-waste, you must:

  • Find a retail or non-profit drop-off location accepting e-waste near you
  • Attend a Community E-Waste Event
  • Visit a Special Waste Drop-Off site
  • Bring it to a SAFE Disposal event
  • Enroll in ecycleNYC (apartment buildings with 10 or more units)
  • Use a manufacturer’s takeback program

Businesses and Nonprofits

Small businesses (fewer than 50 full time employees) and nonprofits (fewer than 75 full time employees) are eligible for free electronics recycling provided by manufacturers. Find out more about manufacturer recycling programs.

Larger businesses must hire an electronic waste recycler. Find out more about electronics handling of businesses.

Schools

Schools must follow specific guidelines for disposal of e-waste. For more information on electronics disposal for NYC DOE Schools, contact WasteSupport@schools.nyc.gov.

NYC Agencies

NYC mayoral agencies are required to contract with a private vendor for safe handling of harmful or hazardous waste. Find out more about the City's Agency Safe Handling contract.

title: Lost and Found Pets
html_text:
Health311Search all NYC.gov websites
MenuPromoting and Protecting the City's Health
Text-Size

  • Business

Lost and Found Pets

  • Birth/Death Records
  • Clinics
  • Vaccine Records
  • Child Care
  • Dog Licenses
  • Lost and Found Pets
  • Food Establishment Inspections
  • Rat Prevention: Training
  • Texting
  • Health Care Price Comparison
Share
Print

How can you make it easier to find your lost pet?

Make sure your pets are wearing a valid license at all times (dogs only) and identification or a microchip.

All dogs in New York City must be licensed

  • If your dog is not currently licensed, see how you can license your dog.
  • If you already have a licensed dog, make sure your contact information is up to date.

If You Find a Lost Pet

If an animal is acting in a threatening or dangerous manner, protect yourself and call 911 immediately. Do not try to handle or physically restrain an aggressive or fearful dog or cat. If an animal appears sick or injured, call 311. Do not try to make physical contact.

If you find a dog that has a New York City dog license tag, use the NYC Dog eLocator to help reunite them with the owner. Enter the 7-digit number on the dog license tag, your name, email or telephone number.

An email will be sent to the dog’s registered owner asking them to contact you. An email is also sent to the NYC Department of Health, which will try to contact the owner by telephone or mail.

If the animal has identification, you can also contact the owner directly. Identification comes in many forms, so please check the animal thoroughly for a tag or-tattoo. You can bring an animal to most shelters and veterinary hospitals to scan the animal for a microchip.

If you find a pet (with or without identification), you also can file a report online or bring the animal to a shelter.

If You Lost Your Pet

Your pet may have been brought to an Animal Care & Control (ACC) shelter. You can file a lost pet report, and you should check ACC's website daily for new lost and stray arrivals. If you see an animal that fits the description of your lost pet, you must visit the identified shelter to reclaim your pet.

You may not recognize your pet from the pictures and descriptions posted online. Consider visiting a few shelters to see for yourself if your pet is there. Find out shelter hours and locations.

  • Look for your pet right away. Check everywhere, especially places that your pet has been before (for example, parks and neighbors' yards/bushes). Search your neighborhood during the day AND at night.
  • Put signs around your neighborhood, in local stores (especially pet stores and grocery stores), and in veterinarians' offices (where allowed) for a 3-mile radius. Your signs should be large enough to be read from 10 feet away.
  • Tell your neighbors (including all neighborhood children), your postal carrier and sanitation workers that your pet is missing.
  • Visit Petfinder.com to check listings of local shelters and rescue groups in your area. The website will post ads for your lost pet.
  • Keep searching for at least a few months. People sometimes find a pet and keep it for a while before taking it to a shelter or abandoning it on the street where they found it. Lost pets have been reunited with their families after long periods of time – don't give up.

Other Tips

Make sure:

  • Your dog has a current license tag attached to its collar or halter.
  • Your pet always wears their collar or halter and that identification tags are securely attached.
  • Identification tags have current information. 
  • If your pet has a microchip, keep your personal information up to date with the company that services the microchip.

title: COVID-19: Vaccine
html_text:
Health311Search all NYC.gov websites
MenuPromoting and Protecting the City's Health
Text-Size
Search

Promoting and Protecting the City's Health

  • Home
  • COVID
  • About
  • Our Health
  • Services
  • Providers
  • Data
  • Business

COVID-19: Vaccine

  • COVID-19
  • Vaccine
  • Data
  • Prevention and Groups at Higher Risk
  • When You Are Sick
  • Long COVID
  • Information for Providers
Share
Print

Updated COVID-19 Vaccines for the 2025-2026 Season Now Available

In New York State, anyone who wants a COVID-19 vaccine can get one. COVID-19 vaccines are covered by most insurance plans, including Medicaid and Medicare.

COVID-19 vaccines are proven to reduce the risk of severe complications from COVID-19, including hospitalization, death, and long COVID.

These recommendations are supported by leading medical organizations, including the American Academy of Family Physicians, the American Academy of Pediatrics, and the American College of Obstetrics and Gynecology.

Anyone 6 months and older should get a COVID-19 vaccine. However, it is especially important for the following groups to get an updated COVID-19 vaccine:

  • All adults ages 65 and older
  • All children ages 6 to 23 months
  • People who are pregnant or recently gave birth
  • People of any age who have an underlying condition that increases their risk for severe COVID-19

The following groups should also get vaccinated:

  • People at high risk of exposure, such as health care workers and people living or working in a nursing home or other congregate setting
  • People living with someone with an underlying condition that increases their risk for severe COVID-19
  • People who have never been vaccinated before

Find a COVID-19 Vaccine

You should ask your provider or pharmacy if they offer the vaccine and talk to your provider if you have questions about whether you should get vaccinated. You should also check with your health plan to make sure COVID-19 vaccinations are covered and find out where to get vaccinated.

Get Vaccinated Today

COVID-19 vaccines remain our best protection against COVID-19. Even healthy people can become very sick from COVID-19 and should be vaccinated. People who have already had COVID-19 should also get vaccinated.

Locations offering 2025–2026 COVID-19 vaccines are available on the NYC Health Map. You can also call your pharmacy or health care provider to see if they have the vaccine in stock.

If you get COVID-19, vaccines reduce your risk of long-term health effects, including long COVID. Even people who have had COVID-19 should get vaccinated.

The vaccines now available are designed to protect against newer variants. They also boost your protection against the virus, which is important as protection lessens over time after vaccination.

Everyone should stay up to date with their vaccines. For most people, that means getting one updated 2025–2026 COVID-19 vaccine dose.

Available Vaccines

Four updated vaccines are available in the US:

  • Spikevax (Moderna) — mRNA vaccine for ages 6 months and older
  • mNexspike (Moderna) — mRNA vaccine, an updated vaccine with lower dosage for ages 12 and older
  • Comirnaty (Pfizer) — mRNA vaccines for ages 5 and older
  • Nuvaxovid (Novavax) — protein-based vaccine for ages 12 and older

You can choose which vaccine to get. Parents can choose for their children as long as the vaccine is available for their child’s age group. It does not matter which vaccine type you have gotten before. Ask your provider or pharmacy if they offer the vaccine and what brands they have available.

Schedule

Most people ages 2 years and older need just one updated vaccine dose this year.

Children ages 6 months to two years who have never been vaccinated against COVID-19 should get two doses of the Spikevax (Moderna) vaccine, four to eight weeks apart. Children who have received a prior dose should get just one Spikevax dose.

If you recently had COVID-19, you may consider waiting three months before getting your next vaccine dose. You may want to get vaccinated sooner if you are at higher risk of severe illness from COVID-19 or getting it again. Talk to your provider about when you should get your next vaccine.

Additional Resources

  • COVID-19 Vaccines Are Still Our Best Defense (PDF)

Most people ages 2 years and older need just one updated vaccine dose this year.
Children ages 6 months to two years who have never been vaccinated against COVID-19 should get two doses of the Spikevax (Moderna) vaccine, four to eight weeks apart. Children who have received a prior dose should get just one Spikevax dose.
If you recently had COVID-19, you may consider waiting three months before getting your next vaccine dose. You may want to get vaccinated sooner if you are at higher risk of severe illness from COVID-19 or getting it again. Talk to your provider about when you should get your next vaccine.

  <script>
   const isSearchPage = document.body.classList.contains("searchpage");
   const isMainSite = document.body.classList.contains("main-site");
   const customSearchBox = document.querySelector('#coveo-search-btn-redirect custom-search-box');
   const mainHost = customSearchBox?.getAttribute("data-search-url");
   if (!isMainSite) {
     const searchBoxes = document.querySelectorAll('custom-search-box[data-search-url]');
     const url = new URL(window.location.href);
     const host = url.host;
     const pathSegments = url.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean);
     const isoCodeRegex = /^[a-z]{2}(-[A-Z]{2})?$/;
     const isSameHost = mainHost.includes(host);
     const searchPageHost = new URL(mainHost).host;
     const isSameDomain = (host === searchPageHost);
     let site = host;
     if (isSameHost) {
       const relevantSegment = isoCodeRegex.test(pathSegments[0]) ? pathSegments[1] : pathSegments[0] || '';
       site = host + "/" + relevantSegment;
     }
     customSearchBox.setAttribute('is-external-domain', !isSameDomain);
     searchBoxes.forEach(box => {
       const currentUrl = box.getAttribute('data-search-url');
       const newUrl = currentUrl + "?sitesearch=" + site;
       box.setAttribute('data-search-url', newUrl);
     });
   }
   if (isSearchPage) {
     const redirectEl = document.getElementById("coveo-search-btn-redirect");
     if (redirectEl) { redirectEl.remove(); }
   } else {
     const resultsEl = document.getElementById("coveo-search-btn-results");
     if (resultsEl) { resultsEl.remove(); }
   }
  </script>
